response.justification: The candidate is a capable full-stack engineer with strong communication skills and a craftsmanship mindset, but the profile is a poor match for this role. C++ experience is minimal and dated (some C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI and a university thesis), with the bulk of their career in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile work. There is no Windows-platform C++ depth, no SQL Server/Oracle experience, and no fixed-income or capital-markets/investment-banking domain exposure (the one 'investment practices' role used Node/JS/GraphQL, not C++).
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience with strong testing/CI/CD discipline
Excellent communication evidenced by talks, workshops, and course creation
Comfortable across many languages and platforms (web, mobile, embedded)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and outdated; no recent hands-on C++ on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience and no fixed-income/capital-markets domain knowledge
Career trajectory centers on TypeScript/Node and DLang, far from the role's core stack
